concerns.text: T029a defines the `SimulationRun` schema with fields: `network_id`, `seed`, `diffusion_rate`, `topology_class`, `steps_run`, `status`. This schema omits the `algorithm` and `parameter_values` required by Constitution Principle VI (Synthetic Data Transparency) to link simulation results back to the specific graph generation provenance. The task fails to preserve the constitutional requirement for full traceability.

T028 (Simulation runner) and T029 (Serialization) do not include a step to capture or log the execution duration (wall-clock time) per network. SC-002 requires measuring simulation runtime against a '≤1 hour per network' constraint. Without a task to record this metric, the success criterion is unmeasurable, violating the requirement that all SCs must have a corresponding verification step.

T035b (Sensitivity sweep) is marked as incomplete/unfinished in the task list (checkbox unchecked). FR-008 and SC-005 mandate a sensitivity sweep across ≥5 distinct clustering coefficient cutoffs. The absence of a completed task to implement this logic and save the required `sensitivity_sweep.json` means a critical functional requirement and success criterion are not preserved in the task plan.

T044 (Statistical power analysis) is marked as incomplete/unfinished. SC-003 requires measuring statistical power against a design target. Without a completed task to generate the `power_analysis_report.json`, the project cannot verify if the sample size meets the power requirements, violating the success criterion.

T037 (Master analysis script) is marked as incomplete/unfinished. FR-006 mandates multiple-comparison correction (Bonferroni/BH) for all hypothesis tests. T037 is the aggregation point that must execute the logic from T034b and save `final_results.json`. Its incomplete status means the requirement for applying and recording these corrections is not preserved in the deliverable plan.
